author: Wenyi
description: Discuss, translate, and rewrite your notes with AI — grounded in the paragraph you are editing.  Answers become foldable callouts and follow-up questions chain. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Wenyi
manifest.description: Discuss, translate, and rewrite your notes with AI — grounded in the paragraph you're editing.
manifest.name: coo

name: coo

releases.author: jwy600
releases.description:
Fixes the CSS performance warning from the Obsidian submission checker. No feature or behavior changes.

- Replaced the three `:has()` selectors in `styles.css` with a plain class (`.coo-composer-open`) that's added to the modal container in `onOpen()`. Identical styling and specificity, without the broad selector invalidation the checker flags.

**Full changelog**: https://github.com/jwy600/obsidian-coo/compare/1.5.3...1.5.4

releases.author: jwy600
releases.description:
Plugin-wide fixes to pass the Obsidian submission lint review. No feature changes.

- **Network**: switched from `fetch` to Obsidian's `requestUrl` (required by the review). Error handling is unchanged — 4xx/5xx bodies are still read and mapped to notices.
- **Lint**: removed all inline `eslint-disable` directives for `obsidianmd/ui/sentence-case` and `no-restricted-globals`; brand/format strings (`coo`, `sk-…`, `GPT-5.6`, `OpenAI`, `Translate`) are now exempted via config instead.
- **Commands**: command IDs no longer include the plugin id (`coo-discuss` → `discuss`, `coo-translate` → `translate`, `coo-re-register` → `re-register`); command names capitalized. ⚠️ If you bound hotkeys to the old `coo:coo-…` command IDs, rebind them after upgrading.
- **Compatibility**: `window.setTimeout` for popout-window support.

**Full changelog**: https://github.com/jwy600/obsidian-coo/compare/1.5.2...1.5.3

releases.author: jwy600
releases.description:
Renames the plugin id from `obsidian-coo` to **`coo`**.

Install into `<Vault>/.obsidian/plugins/coo/`. The GitHub repo URL is unchanged.

If you installed an earlier release under the `obsidian-coo` folder, remove it and install `coo` fresh — plugin settings (including your API key) are keyed to the id and don't carry over. The id `coo` is free in the Obsidian community plugin directory.

No behavior changes from 1.5.1.

releases.author: jwy600
releases.description:
Small follow-up to 1.5.0.

## Added
- **Selection highlight** — opening Discuss on a selected word wraps it in a `==highlight==` in the note, so the focal term stays visible. The callout title is the *question*, not the word, so the highlight records what was selected. It persists after the composer closes.

## Fixed
- Selecting a word in a list item and asking no longer swallows the following list item into the callout. A trailing blank line now keeps the callout its own block, so a tight-list item (e.g. `3. ...`) that followed it stays visible in read mode.

Upgrade by replacing `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` with the assets below.

releases.author: jwy600
releases.description:
First public release of coo for Obsidian.

coo brings AI into the paragraph you're editing — discuss a passage, translate inline, and fold answers back in. The composer is the command bar; your note is the canvas.

## Features
- **Discuss** — ask questions about a paragraph (your highlighted phrase is the focal point). Answers save as collapsed `[!coo]` callouts below the paragraph, and follow-up questions chain so each builds on the last.
  - **Drill down** — select a phrase inside an answer and ask again; the follow-up stacks right beneath it.
  - **Whole-document mode** — run Discuss with nothing selected to ask about the whole note.
  - **Pre-filled question** — the Ask box comes with a localized default question (e.g. "What does this mean?"); press Ask to send it.
  - **[Minor] tag** — skippable concepts are flagged in the callout title.
  - **Rewrite** — fold gathered answers back into the paragraph and remove the callouts.
- **Translate** — insert an inline translation in parentheses right after the selected word or phrase.
- **Re-register note** — refresh the chaining snapshot after heavy edits.

## Requirements
- Obsidian 1.13.1 or later
- An OpenAI API key

See the README for setup and settings.

releases.author: jwy600
releases.description:
## What's new

- **5-language support**: English, Spanish, French, Chinese, and Japanese for both response and translation languages
- **Auto-detect locale**: Response language is automatically set from your Obsidian locale on first use
- **Smart language conflict prevention**: Translation language dropdown excludes your response language and auto-adjusts on conflict
- **Flat prompt folder**: Prompt files now live in a single `prompts/` folder (no per-language subfolders). Existing files are migrated automatically
- **New `atomic.md` prompt**: A concise, atomic-note style prompt option alongside the default `knowledgeassistant.md`
- **Custom system prompts**: Add your own `.md` files to `prompts/` with optional `<language>` tag for automatic i18n
- **Runtime language injection**: All prompts are language-neutral on disk; language directives are applied at runtime based on your settings

releases.author: jwy600
releases.description:
## Bug fixes

- **Fix highlight style leaking into new text** — After picking phrases in the Coo Discuss composer, clearing the content area (Ctrl+A → Backspace) no longer causes newly typed text to inherit the highlight style. Empty `<span class="coo-picked">` nodes are now cleaned up automatically.

releases.author: jwy600
releases.description:
Initial release of Obsidian Coo — AI-powered block-level annotation and rewriting for your notes.

## Features
- **Coo: Ask** — ask a question, get an AI response saved as a new note
- **Coo: Discuss** — select text and chat with AI in a composer modal with quick actions (Translate, Example, Expand, ELI5)
- **Coo: Rewrite** — rewrite a paragraph incorporating picked annotations
- **Coo: Inspire** — expand `{instruction}` inline into AI-generated bullet points
- Phrase picking via drag-select with `%%...%%` annotation format
- Editor context menu integration
- Configurable model, reasoning effort, web search, language, and system prompt
